prompt:
Perf ticket, ingest tier. Package telewire (this repo) encodes telemetry readings into our pipe-delimited line frames — AppendFrame(dst, reading) is called for every sample on every collector, tens of millions of frames an hour, and the profiler says the encoder itself is now a top-3 allocation site fleet-wide. The current code formats each frame through Sprintf plus a chain of string replacements, so every single frame burns half a dozen heap allocations and the GC eats our headroom during bursts.

The API is already append-style precisely so this can be allocation-free on the hot path: callers hand in a reused buffer and take back the extended slice. Make the encoder live up to that. In steady state (buffer already grown), encoding a batch must not allocate per frame — the acceptance suite pins this with a testing.AllocsPerRun budget of 8 allocations for a 512-frame batch, which is huge headroom over the 0-1 a buffer-reusing encoder needs and hopelessly small for anything that allocates per frame.

The wire format is frozen and the tests pin it byte-for-byte: <ts>|<device>|<metric>|<value>|<ff>\n, decimal ts/value with negatives allowed, exactly two lowercase hex digits for flags, and the four escape rules for device/metric ('\' -> \\, '|' -> \|, newline -> \n, carriage return -> \r). ErrEmptyDevice / ErrEmptyMetric identities stay, dst comes back unchanged on error, and concurrent callers with their own buffers must stay safe (no sneaky package-level scratch state — the race detector is on).

go test -race -timeout 120s ./... is the acceptance contract; telewire_test.go is off limits.
